tables.name: fact_order_masterdata_upiv
tables.description: The fact_order_masterdata_upiv table contains data related to orders and notifications in the business. It includes information such as order numbers, client details, order types, descriptions, plant numbers, and object numbers. Additionally, it includes processing times for orders and notification details such as notification numbers, types, and texts. The table also includes malfunction start and end dates, times, and timestamps. This table serves as a central repository for order and notification data, providing valuable insights into the business operations and performance.

measures.name: Selected KPI Value
measures.expression:
VAR SelectedIdx = SELECTEDVALUE('KPI Selector'[Value3], 0)
RETURN
    SWITCH(
        SelectedIdx,
        0, [confiremd_phc_exception_agg],
        1, [country_gross_revenue_doc],
        2, [total_samplling],
        3, [logistcs_fee_upcharge],
        4, [confiremd_phc],
        BLANK()
    )

source.expression:
{
    ("Confirmed PHC", NAMEOF('base_exception_agg'[confiremd_phc_exception_agg]), 0),
    ("Country Gross Revenue", NAMEOF('doc_exception_agg'[country_gross_revenue_doc]), 1),
    ("Total Sampling", NAMEOF('mat_exception_agg'[total_samplling]), 2),
    ("Logistics Fee", NAMEOF('base_const_sel'[logistcs_fee_upcharge]), 3),
    ("Base Combi PHC", NAMEOF('base_combi'[confiremd_phc]), 4)
}

columns.description: Sum of annualized revenue across all leases

columns.description: Monthly revenue from colocation agreements

columns.description: Sum of projected lifetime value of all leases

columns.description: Count of currently active leases

columns.description: Sum of all antennas across towers

measures.name: Failed Checks VS PW
measures.expression:
VAR CurrentWeek = SELECTEDVALUE(dim_weeks[Week])
VAR CurrentWeekFails = SUM(f_customer_aggregated_l1_l2[Failed Checks])
VAR PreviousWeek = CurrentWeek - 1
VAR PreviousWeekFails = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(f_customer_aggregated_l1_l2[Failed Checks]),
        REMOVEFILTERS(dim_weeks[Week]),
        dim_weeks[Week] = PreviousWeek
    )
RETURN
    IF(
        NOT ISBLANK(PreviousWeekFails),
        CurrentWeekFails - PreviousWeekFails,
        BLANK()
    )

source.expression:
let
    Source = dc_datalake_prod_001("
        SELECT 
            fca.country_code,
            fca.cust_class_id,
            fca.rule_code,
            fca.failed_checks,
            fca.passed_checks,
            fca.skipped_checks,
            fca.total_checks,
            fca.loading_date
        FROM 
            application_data_quality_ca_data_control_center_golden.f_customer_aggregated_l1_l2 AS fca
        LEFT JOIN
            curated_data_quality_data_control_center.conf_country_rule_exception AS exc
            ON fca.country_code = exc.country_code 
            AND fca.rule_code = exc.rule_code
        WHERE
            fca.loading_date >= " & StartingDate & "
            AND exc.process_run_id IS NULL
        "
    ),
    #"Renamed Columns" = Table.RenameColumns(Source,{{"failed_checks", "Failed Checks"}, {"passed_checks", "Passed Checks"}, {"skipped_checks", "Skipped Checks"}, {"total_checks", "Total Checks"}})

columns.name: is_latest_date_in_week
columns.expression:
VAR CurrentDate = dim_weeks[loading_date]
VAR CurrentWeek = dim_weeks[Week]
VAR MaxDateInWeek = 
    CALCULATE(
        MAX(dim_weeks[loading_date]),
        FILTER(
            ALL(dim_weeks),
            dim_weeks[Week] = CurrentWeek
        )
    )
RETURN
    IF(CurrentDate = MaxDateInWeek, 1, 0)

measures.name: Numbers VS PW (latest universe)
measures.expression:
VAR CurrentWeek = SELECTEDVALUE(dim_weeks[Week])
VAR CurrentWeekNums = SUM(tbl_approximate_universe[num_customers])
VAR PreviousWeek = CurrentWeek - 1
VAR PreviousWeekNums = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(tbl_approximate_universe[num_customers]),
        REMOVEFILTERS(dim_weeks[Week]),
        dim_weeks[Week] = PreviousWeek
    )
RETURN
    IF(
        NOT ISBLANK(PreviousWeekNums),
        CurrentWeekNums - PreviousWeekNums,
        BLANK()
    )

measures.name: Numbers VS PW (latest universe) excl. full universe attr
measures.expression:
VAR CurrentAttribute = SELECTEDVALUE(tbl_approximate_universe[attribute_name])
RETURN
    SWITCH(
        CurrentAttribute,
        "full_universe",
            BLANK(),
        [Numbers VS PW (latest universe)]
    )

measures.name: Ratios (latest universe)
measures.expression:
VAR wo_complete_cgr = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(tbl_approximate_universe[num_customers]),
        tbl_approximate_universe[attribute_name] = "customer_wo_complete_CGR"
    )
VAR wo_perfect_cgr = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(tbl_approximate_universe[num_customers]),
        tbl_approximate_universe[attribute_name] = "customer_wo_perfect_CGR"
    )
VAR full_universe = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(tbl_approximate_universe[num_customers]),
        tbl_approximate_universe[attribute_name] = "full_universe"
    )
VAR CurrentAttribute = SELECTEDVALUE(tbl_approximate_universe[attribute_name])
RETURN
    SWITCH(
        CurrentAttribute,
        "customer_wo_complete_CGR", 
            DIVIDE(wo_complete_cgr, full_universe, BLANK()),
        "customer_wo_perfect_CGR",
            DIVIDE(wo_perfect_cgr, full_universe, BLANK()),
        BLANK()
    )

source.expression:
let
    Source = #"dc_adb-landing-zone-002"("SELECT * FROM `dc_adb-landing-zone-002`.dcc_monitoring.tbl_approximate_universe")

expressions.name: dim_rules_inventory_email_and_phone
expressions.expression:
let
    Source = dc_datalake_prod_001("
        SELECT 
            rule_code,
            business_attribute_name,
            domain,
            sub_domain,
            quality_category,
            is_field_mandatory,
            rule_description,
            CONCAT('0000_', business_attribute_name) AS new_order_business_attribute
        FROM 
            curated_data_quality_data_control_center.dim_rules_inventory
        WHERE
            (is_active = 1 OR rule_code IN ('RCCOMP_54.1', 'RCCOMP_52.1', 'RCCOMP_50.1'))
            AND domain = 'Customer'
            AND business_attribute_name IN ('E-mail, Mobile Phone', 'E-mail, Mobile Phone, Phone', 'Mobile Phone', 'E-mail', 'E-Mail', 'Any Phone number')
        "
    )
in
    Source

expressions.name: dim_rules_inventory_all_other
expressions.expression:
let
    Source = dc_datalake_prod_001("
        SELECT 
            rule_code,
            'All Other' AS business_attribute_name,
            domain,
            sub_domain,
            quality_category,
            is_field_mandatory,
            rule_description,
            '9999_All Other' AS new_order_business_attribute
        FROM 
            curated_data_quality_data_control_center.dim_rules_inventory
        WHERE
            (is_active = 1 OR rule_code IN ('RCCOMP_54.1', 'RCCOMP_52.1', 'RCCOMP_50.1'))
            AND domain = 'Customer'
            AND business_attribute_name NOT IN ('E-mail, Mobile Phone', 'E-mail, Mobile Phone, Phone', 'Mobile Phone', 'E-mail', 'E-Mail', 'Any Phone number')
        "
    )
in
    Source

tables.name: bom
tables.description: The table contains information about the bill of materials (BOM) used in the manufacturing supply chain. It includes details on the materials used in production, the quantities, and any rescued data. This table can be used to analyze material requirements, track inventory levels, and optimize production processes.

columns.name: material_in
columns.description: Represents the type of material that is input into the manufacturing process, which is essential for understanding production needs.

columns.name: material_out
columns.description: Indicates the type of material that results from the manufacturing process, useful for tracking final products and inventory outputs.

columns.name: qty
columns.description: Denotes the quantity of materials involved in the bill of materials, critical for inventory management and production planning.

columns.name: _rescued_data
columns.description: Contains any additional retrieved information relevant to the materials, which may aid in data recovery or correction processes.

tables.name: shipment_recommendations
tables.description: The table contains recommendations for shipping products based on various parameters. It includes details such as the product type, the plant from which it is shipped, the distribution center involved, and the quantity shipped. This data can be used to optimize shipping strategies, identify patterns in supply chain operations, and improve overall efficiency in logistics.

columns.name: product
columns.description: Information about the type of product that is being recommended for shipment, which can guide inventory and logistics decisions.

columns.name: plant
columns.description: Identifies the specific manufacturing plant from which the product is being shipped, offering insight into production sources and capabilities.

columns.name: distribution_center
columns.description: Details the distribution center involved in handling the shipment, key for tracking logistics and distribution processes.

columns.name: qty_shipped
columns.description: Indicates the quantity of the product that is recommended to be shipped, essential for inventory management and planning logistics.

columns.name: _rescued_data
columns.description: Contains any additional or corrected information that was retrieved during the data processing, useful for maintaining data integrity and understanding anomalies.

tables.name: employment_action_plan_demo_data
tables.description: The table contains data related to employment action plans in the Province of Ontario, focusing on various demographic and program-specific details. It includes information such as client identifiers, program names, referral statuses, and demographic indicators like age and immigration status. Possible use cases for this table include analyzing the demographics of clients served, tracking the progress and outcomes of employment programs, and evaluating the effectiveness of various referral paths and services provided.

columns.name: est_catchment_area
columns.description: The geographical area served by the specific employment program, which can influence the demographic characteristics of clients.

columns.name: ssm_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for the service support manager responsible for overseeing the employment action plan.

columns.name: ssm_name
columns.description: The name of the service support manager linked to the employment action plan.

columns.name: sds_reference_number
columns.description: An identifier used for tracking specific service delivery supports associated with each employment action plan.

columns.name: sds_name
columns.description: The name of the service delivery support linked to the employment action plan.

columns.name: sds_postal_code
columns.description: The postal code associated with the service delivery support, useful for geographic analysis.

columns.name: prg_name
columns.description: The name of the program under which the employment action plan is categorized.

columns.name: reference_number
columns.description: An overall reference number associated with the employment action plan for tracking and management purposes.

columns.name: sams_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for the client in the Service and Activity Management System.

columns.name: casereference
columns.description: A unique reference number for the case associated with the employment action plan.

columns.name: stream
columns.description: The specific stream or category within the employment program that the client falls under.

columns.name: score
columns.description: A calculated score that may reflect the client's eligibility or suitability for the program based on various criteria.

columns.name: est_ca_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for the estimated catchment area associated with the employment action plan.

columns.name: referral_indicator
columns.description: A mark indicating whether the client has been referred to a particular program or service.

columns.name: referred_program
columns.description: The name of the program to which the client has been referred, if applicable.

columns.name: startdate
columns.description: The date on which the client began participating in the employment action plan.

columns.name: status
columns.description: The current status of the employment action plan, indicating progress or changes over time.

columns.name: client_city
columns.description: The city where the client resides, which may help in understanding the geographic distribution of clients.

columns.name: aborigfirstnations
columns.description: An indicator representing whether or not the client identified themselves as a member of First Nations among the Indigenous peoples of Canada.

columns.name: aborigmetis
columns.description: An indicator representing whether or not the client identified themselves as Métis among the Indigenous peoples of Canada.

columns.name: aboriginuit
columns.description: An indicator representing whether or not the client identified themselves as Inuit among the Indigenous peoples of Canada.

columns.name: immigrated_to_canada
columns.description: A status indicating whether the client has immigrated to Canada, highlighting immigrant demographics.

columns.name: canada_status
columns.description: The immigration status of the client in Canada, which may affect their eligibility for certain programs.

columns.name: age
columns.description: The age of the client, providing insight into the demographic profile of those served.

columns.name: datearrivedincanada
columns.description: The date on which the client arrived in Canada, relevant for understanding their integration timeline.

columns.name: years_in_canada
columns.description: The number of years the client has lived in Canada, which may influence their access to programs and services.

columns.name: visibleminorityind
columns.description: An indicator representing whether the client belongs to a visible minority group.

columns.name: disabilityind
columns.description: An indicator showing whether the client has a recognized disability.

columns.name: newcomerind
columns.description: An indicator of whether the client is classified as a newcomer to Canada.

columns.name: francophoneind
columns.description: An indicator representing whether the client is a Francophone, highlighting language demographics.

columns.name: odsp_ind
columns.description: An indicator indicating if the client is receiving assistance from the Ontario Disability Support Program.

columns.name: dis_ind
columns.description: An indicator for disabilities, useful in evaluating the clients' needs for tailored support.

columns.name: source_of_income
columns.description: The primary source of income for the client, providing insight into financial circumstances.

columns.name: enddate
columns.description: The date when the client’s participation in the employment action plan is expected to conclude.

columns.name: closure_reason
columns.description: The reason for the closure of the client's case in the employment action plan.

columns.name: refer_in
columns.description: Indicates whether the client has been referred from an external agency or source.

columns.name: phase
columns.description: The current phase of the employment action plan that the client is engaged in.

columns.name: referral_path
columns.description: The specific pathway through which the client was referred into the employment action plan.

columns.name: referral_true
columns.description: An indicator confirming the legitimacy of the referral for the client to the program.

columns.name: soi_type
columns.description: The type of source of income, denoting the financial backgrounds of clients.

columns.name: youth24
columns.description: An indicator representing whether the client falls under the youth category, particularly those aged 24 and under.

columns.name: exitdate
columns.description: The date when the client officially exits the employment action plan.

columns.name: exitflag
columns.description: A flag indicating whether the exit was voluntary or involuntary.

columns.name: client_served
columns.description: An indicator denoting whether the client has received services under the employment action plan.

columns.name: revenue

tables.name: early_education_data
tables.description: The 'early_education_data' table contains information related to student assessments, attendance, program participation, and regional data. It serves as a comprehensive record of student performance and engagement in educational activities. The data in this table can be used to analyze student progress, identify trends in academic achievement, and assess the effectiveness of educational programs. Additionally, it provides insights into regional variations in educational outcomes and participation rates. This table is valuable for educational institutions and policymakers to make informed decisions and improve early education initiatives.

columns.name: school_id

columns.name: assessment_date

columns.name: reading_score
columns.description: Score achieved in reading assessment

columns.name: math_score

columns.name: attendance_rate
columns.description: Rate of attendance for the student

columns.name: program_participation
columns.description: Indicates if the student is participating in a specific program

columns.name: region
columns.description: Region where the school is located

columns.description: Unique identifier for each claim record

tables.name: fact_patient_claims
tables.description: The table contains data related to patient claims, including details about the claims submitted, associated physicians, and payment amounts. It can be used for analyzing claim trends, understanding payment patterns, and evaluating the performance of healthcare providers. Key information includes claim identifiers, dates, diagnosis and procedure codes, and payment details.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each claim submitted, allowing for tracking and referencing specific claims in the dataset.

columns.name: beneficiary_key
columns.description: Identifier for the patient or beneficiary associated with the claim, essential for linking claims to individual patients.

columns.name: claim_type
columns.description: Indicates the type of claim submitted, which can help in categorizing and analyzing different claim scenarios.

columns.name: attending_physician_provider_key
columns.description: Identifier for the physician who attended to the patient, providing insight into the healthcare providers involved in the claim.

columns.name: operating_physician_provider_key
columns.description: Identifier for the physician who performed any surgical procedures, important for understanding the roles of different providers in the claim.

columns.name: other_physician_provider_key
columns.description: Identifier for any additional physicians involved in the patient's care, allowing for a comprehensive view of the healthcare team.

columns.name: claim_line_segment
columns.description: Represents the line item number within the claim, useful for detailed analysis of multiple services or procedures included in a single claim.

columns.name: claim_start_date
columns.description: The date when the claim service began, which is crucial for understanding the timeline of care and services provided.

columns.name: claim_end_date
columns.description: The date when the claim service ended, helping to establish the duration of care and the timing of services rendered.

columns.name: inpatient_admission_date
columns.description: Date of the patient's admission to a healthcare facility, relevant for inpatient claims and understanding the context of care.

columns.name: claim_payment_amount
columns.description: Total amount paid for the claim, providing insight into the financial aspects of the claim and payment processes.

columns.name: primary_payer_claim_paid_amount
columns.description: Amount paid by the primary payer for the claim, essential for analyzing payment sources and financial responsibility.

columns.name: diagnosis_key_1
columns.description: Code representing the primary diagnosis associated with the claim, important for understanding the medical conditions being treated.

columns.name: diagnosis_key_2
columns.description: Code for a secondary diagnosis, allowing for a more comprehensive view of the patient's health status and treatment needs.

columns.name: diagnosis_key_3
columns.description: Code for a tertiary diagnosis, further detailing the patient's medical conditions relevant to the claim.

columns.name: diagnosis_key_4
columns.description: Code for an additional diagnosis, contributing to a complete picture of the patient's health and treatment.

columns.name: diagnosis_key_5
columns.description: Code for another diagnosis, ensuring all relevant health issues are captured for the claim.

columns.name: procedure_key_1
columns.description: Code for the primary procedure performed, critical for understanding the services rendered in relation to the claim.

columns.name: procedure_key_2
columns.description: Code for a secondary procedure, providing additional context on the treatments provided to the patient.

columns.name: procedure_key_3
columns.description: Code for a tertiary procedure, further detailing the interventions performed during the claim period.

columns.name: procedure_key_4
columns.description: Code for an additional procedure, ensuring all relevant services are documented for comprehensive analysis.

columns.name: procedure_key_5
columns.description: Code for another procedure, capturing any further interventions that may impact the claim.

columns.name: admitting_key_1
columns.description: Identifier for the admitting physician or provider, important for understanding who initiated the patient's care.

tables.name: dim_beneficiary
tables.description: The table contains demographic and health-related information about beneficiaries. It includes details such as date of birth, gender, race, and various health conditions. This data can be used for analyzing beneficiary characteristics, understanding healthcare coverage patterns, and evaluating the financial aspects of Medicare reimbursements. Potential use cases include research on health disparities, assessing the impact of chronic conditions on healthcare costs, and improving service delivery for specific beneficiary groups.

columns.name: beneficiary_key
columns.description: A unique identifier for each beneficiary, allowing for tracking and analysis of individual health data.

columns.name: beneficiary_code
columns.description: A code that represents the beneficiary, which may be used for internal classification or reporting purposes.

columns.name: date_of_birth
columns.description: The birth date of the beneficiary, essential for age-related analyses and demographic studies.

columns.name: date_of_death
columns.description: The date when the beneficiary passed away, useful for mortality studies and understanding health outcomes.

columns.name: gender
columns.description: The gender of the beneficiary, which can be important for analyzing health trends and disparities.

columns.name: race
columns.description: The racial background of the beneficiary, providing insights into demographic diversity and health equity.

columns.name: esrd_flag
columns.description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has End-Stage Renal Disease, which is critical for understanding specific health needs.

columns.name: state
columns.description: The state where the beneficiary resides, useful for geographic analysis of health services and outcomes.

columns.name: county_code
columns.description: A code representing the county of residence, allowing for more localized health data analysis.

columns.name: part_a_coverage_months
columns.description: The number of months the beneficiary has Medicare Part A coverage, relevant for understanding insurance coverage duration.

columns.name: part_b_coverage_months
columns.description: The number of months the beneficiary has Medicare Part B coverage, important for evaluating access to outpatient services.

columns.name: hmo_coverage_months
columns.description: The number of months the beneficiary has been covered by a Health Maintenance Organization, which can affect care delivery.

columns.name: part_d_coverage_months
columns.description: The number of months the beneficiary has Medicare Part D coverage, crucial for analyzing prescription drug access.

columns.name: alzheimers_or_related_flag
columns.description: Indicates if the beneficiary has Alzheimer's disease or related conditions, important for targeted health interventions.

columns.name: heart_failure_flag
columns.description: Shows whether the beneficiary has heart failure, which is significant for understanding chronic disease management.

columns.name: cronic_kidney_disease_flag
columns.description: Indicates the presence of chronic kidney disease, relevant for assessing healthcare needs and costs.

columns.name: cancer_flag
columns.description: Indicates if the beneficiary has been diagnosed with cancer, which is vital for cancer care research and resource allocation.

columns.name: copd_flag
columns.description: Shows whether the beneficiary has Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, important for respiratory health studies.

columns.name: depression_flag
columns.description: Indicates if the beneficiary has been diagnosed with depression, relevant for mental health assessments.

columns.name: diabetes_flag
columns.description: Shows whether the beneficiary has diabetes, which is crucial for understanding chronic disease prevalence and management.

columns.name: ischemic_heart_disease_flag
columns.description: Indicates the presence of ischemic heart disease, important for cardiovascular health analysis.

columns.name: osteoporosis_flag
columns.description: Shows whether the beneficiary has osteoporosis, relevant for studies on bone health and aging.

columns.name: asrheumatoid_arthritis_flag
columns.description: Indicates if the beneficiary has rheumatoid arthritis, important for understanding autoimmune conditions.

columns.name: stroke_transient_ischemic_attack_flag
columns.description: Shows whether the beneficiary has experienced a stroke or transient ischemic attack, critical for stroke-related research.

columns.name: inpatient_annual_medicare_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by Medicare for inpatient services annually, useful for financial analysis.

columns.name: inpatient_annual_beneficiary_responsibility_amount
columns.description: The annual amount the beneficiary is responsible for in inpatient costs, important for understanding out-of-pocket expenses.

columns.name: inpatient_annual_payer_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by other payers for inpatient services annually, relevant for comprehensive financial assessments.

columns.name: outpatient_institutional_annual_medicare_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by Medicare for outpatient institutional services annually, useful for evaluating outpatient care costs.

columns.name: outpatient_institutional_annual_beneficiary_responsibiliy_amount
columns.description: The annual amount the beneficiary is responsible for in outpatient institutional costs, important for financial planning.

columns.name: outpatient_institutional_annual_primary_payer_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by primary payers for outpatient institutional services annually, relevant for understanding overall reimbursement patterns.

columns.name: carrier_annual_medicare_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by Medicare for carrier services annually, useful for analyzing carrier-related costs.

columns.name: carrier_annual_beneficiary_responsiblity_amount
columns.description: The annual amount the beneficiary is responsible for in carrier costs, important for assessing financial impact.

columns.name: carrier_annual_primary_payer_reimbursement_amount
columns.description: The total amount reimbursed by primary payers for carrier services annually, relevant for comprehensive financial evaluations.

columns.name: __START_AT
columns.description: Indicates the starting point for data collection or analysis, useful for time-based studies.

columns.name: __END_AT
columns.description: Indicates the endpoint for data collection or analysis, important for defining the scope of the data.

columns.name: request_date
columns.description: The UTC date on which the model serving request was received.

columns.name: client_request_id
columns.description: The user-provided request identifier that can be specified in the model serving request body.

columns.name: request_time
columns.description: The timestamp at which the request is received.

columns.name: status_code
columns.description: The HTTP status code that was returned from the model.

columns.name: sampling_fraction
columns.description: The sampling fraction used in the event that the request was down-sampled. This value is between 0 and 1, where 1 represents that 100% of incoming requests were included.

columns.name: execution_duration_ms
columns.description: The time in milliseconds for which the model performed inference. This does not include overhead network latencies and only represents the time it took for the model to generate predictions.

columns.name: request
columns.description: The raw request JSON body that was sent to the model serving endpoint.

columns.name: response
columns.description: The raw response JSON body that was returned by the model serving endpoint.

columns.name: logging_error_codes
columns.description: The errors that occurred when the data could not be logged. Error codes include MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_EXCEEDED and MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE_EXCEEDED.

columns.name: served_entity_id
columns.description: The unique ID of the served entity.

columns.name: requester
columns.description: The ID of the user or service principal whose permissions are used for the invocation request of the serving endpoint. This field returns NULL for route-optimized custom model endpoints.

tables.name: fact_claims
tables.description: Claims fact table for healthcare payer

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for each claim submitted for processing in the healthcare system.

columns.name: member_id
columns.description: This identifier represents the member who is associated with the claim, linking it to their profile.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: An identifier for the healthcare provider who rendered services related to the claim.

columns.name: claim_date
columns.description: The date on which the claim was submitted to the payer for processing.

columns.name: service_date
columns.description: The date when the healthcare services were actually provided to the member.

columns.name: diagnosis_code
columns.description: A code representing the diagnosis associated with the claim, typically aligned with standard coding systems.

columns.name: procedure_code
columns.description: A code that identifies the specific medical procedure performed for which the claim is being made.

columns.name: claim_type
columns.description: Medical, Pharmacy, Laboratory, Emergency, Inpatient, Outpatient

columns.name: claim_status
columns.description: Paid, Denied, Pending, Partially Paid

columns.name: claim_amount
columns.description: The total monetary value of the claim submitted by the healthcare provider.

columns.name: paid_amount
columns.description: The amount that has been approved and paid by the payer for the submitted claim.

columns.name: member_responsibility
columns.description: The portion of the claim amount that is the financial responsibility of the member, such as copays or deductibles.

columns.name: denial_reason
columns.description: Details the reason for any denial of claims, providing insight into why a claim was not approved.

tables.name: churn_user_features
tables.description: These features are derived from the churn_bronze_customers table in the lakehouse.  We created dummy variables for the categorical columns, cleaned up their names, and added a boolean flag for whether the customer churned or not.  No aggregations were performed.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for each user, allowing for tracking and analysis of individual customer behavior.

columns.name: canal
columns.description: Indicates the channel through which the customer engaged with the service, useful for understanding customer acquisition sources.

columns.name: country
columns.description: Represents the country of the customer, which can help in analyzing geographical trends and preferences.

columns.name: gender
columns.description: Denotes the gender of the customer, providing insights into demographic patterns and preferences.

columns.name: age_group
columns.description: Categorizes customers into age groups, facilitating demographic analysis and targeted marketing strategies.

columns.name: churn
columns.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the customer has churned, essential for understanding customer retention and attrition rates.

columns.name: order_count
columns.description: The total number of orders placed by the customer, which can be used to assess customer engagement and loyalty.

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Represents the total monetary value of all orders made by the customer, useful for revenue analysis.

columns.name: total_item
columns.description: The total number of items purchased by the customer, providing insights into purchasing behavior and preferences.

columns.name: last_transaction
columns.description: The timestamp of the customer's most recent transaction, important for tracking customer activity and engagement.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: Identifies the platform used by the customer to engage with the service, aiding in understanding user experience across different channels.

columns.name: event_count
columns.description: The total number of events associated with the customer, which can help in analyzing customer interactions and engagement levels.

columns.name: session_count
columns.description: The number of sessions initiated by the customer, useful for understanding usage patterns and frequency of engagement.

columns.name: days_since_creation
columns.description: The number of days since the customer's account was created, providing context for customer lifecycle analysis.

columns.name: days_since_last_activity
columns.description: Indicates the number of days since the customer's last activity, which can help in identifying inactive customers.

columns.name: days_last_event
columns.description: The number of days since the customer's last recorded event, useful for tracking engagement and potential churn risk.

columns.name: total_customers
columns.description: The total number of unique customers in the dataset, as counted by user_id.

columns.name: predicted_churned_customers
columns.description: The total number of customers predicted to churn by the machine learning model (sum of the churn_prediction column).

columns.name: actual_churned_customers
columns.description: The actual number of customers who have churned, according to historical data (sum of the churn column).

columns.name: predicted_churn_rate
columns.description: The proportion of customers predicted to churn, calculated as the sum of churn_prediction divided by total customers.

columns.name: actual_churn_rate
columns.description: The actual proportion of customers who have churned, calculated as the sum of churn divided by total customers.

columns.name: churn_confidence_gap
columns.description: The absolute difference between the actual and predicted churn rates, highlighting how closely the model's predictions align with reality (lower is better).

tables.name: customer_churn_propensity_next_best_action
tables.description: The table contains data related to customer churn analysis. It includes information such as customer names, user IDs, churn propensity scores, current tariffs, payment methods, recommended next actions, consumption patterns, and contract end dates. This data can be used to identify customers at risk of churning, tailor retention strategies, and analyze customer behavior over time.

columns.name: Customer_Name
columns.description: Customer Organisation Name

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: customer id

columns.name: Churn_Propensity_Score
columns.description: The percentage score indicating the likelihood of the customer churning (e.g., 80)

columns.name: Current_Tariff
columns.description: The type of tariff the customer is currently on (e.g., Standard Variable Rate)

columns.name: Payment_Method
columns.description: The method the customer uses to pay their bills (e.g., Direct Debit)

columns.name: Next_Best_Action
columns.description: The recommended action to prevent churn, as suggested by the prediction result (e.g., Offer a 2-year fixed price contract with a 5% discount)

columns.name: Consumption_Pattern
columns.description: A description of the customer's energy usage pattern (e.g., High usage during winter months, low usage during summer months)

columns.name: Contract_End_Date
columns.description: The date when the customer's current contract is set to expire (e.g., 3 months from now)

columns.name: request_date
columns.description: The UTC date on which the model serving request was received.

columns.name: client_request_id
columns.description: The user-provided request identifier that can be specified in the model serving request body.

columns.name: request_time
columns.description: The timestamp at which the request is received.

columns.name: status_code
columns.description: The HTTP status code that was returned from the model.

columns.name: sampling_fraction
columns.description: The sampling fraction used in the event that the request was down-sampled. This value is between 0 and 1, where 1 represents that 100% of incoming requests were included.

columns.name: execution_duration_ms
columns.description: The time in milliseconds for which the model performed inference. This does not include overhead network latencies and only represents the time it took for the model to generate predictions.

columns.name: request
columns.description: The raw request JSON body that was sent to the model serving endpoint.

columns.name: response
columns.description: The raw response JSON body that was returned by the model serving endpoint.

columns.name: logging_error_codes
columns.description: The errors that occurred when the data could not be logged. Error codes include MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_EXCEEDED and MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE_EXCEEDED.

columns.name: served_entity_id
columns.description: The unique ID of the served entity.

columns.name: requester
columns.description: The ID of the user or service principal whose permissions are used for the invocation request of the serving endpoint. This field returns NULL for route-optimized custom model endpoints.

tables.name: vw_demand_vs_actuals
tables.description: Demand forecast accuracy by product and facility. Includes forecasted vs actual demand and model error metrics. Use for S&OP and planning accuracy dashboards in Power BI.

columns.name: forecast_id
columns.description: Unique forecast record identifier.

columns.name: forecast_date
columns.description: The date this forecast is predicting demand for.

columns.name: facility_id
columns.description: Facility identifier. Joins to vw_facility_network.facility_id.

columns.name: facility_name
columns.description: Facility this demand forecast applies to.

columns.name: division
columns.description: Simplot business division responsible for this facility.

columns.name: region
columns.description: Geographic region: Pacific NW, Mountain West, Midwest, Southeast, Europe, or APAC.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: Unique product identifier.

columns.name: product_name
columns.description: Product being forecasted (e.g. Ore-Ida Golden Crinkles, MAP 11-52-0 Fertilizer).

columns.name: product_category
columns.description: Product category: Frozen Potato, Phosphate Fertilizer, Potash, or Crop Protection.

columns.name: unit_of_measure
columns.description: Unit of measure for demand quantities (e.g. cases, tons).

columns.name: model_version
columns.description: ML model version used to generate this forecast (e.g. v2.1-seasonal, v3.0-xgboost).

columns.name: forecasted_demand
columns.description: Predicted demand quantity in product unit of measure for this date and location.

columns.name: confidence_lower
columns.description: Lower bound of the 90% confidence interval for this forecast.

columns.name: confidence_upper
columns.description: Upper bound of the 90% confidence interval for this forecast.

columns.name: confidence_interval_width
columns.description: Width of the confidence interval (upper minus lower). Wider = less certain forecast.

columns.name: actual_demand
columns.description: Actual demand recorded for this product/facility/date. NULL if the date is in the future.

columns.name: forecast_variance
columns.description: Forecasted minus actual. Positive = overforecast (excess inventory risk). Negative = underforecast (stockout risk).

columns.name: forecast_error_pct
columns.description: Forecast error as a percentage of actual demand. Lower is better. NULL for future dates.

columns.name: seasonality_factor
columns.description: Seasonal adjustment applied by the model. Above 1.0 = peak season. Below 1.0 = off-season.

columns.name: forecast_accuracy_band
columns.description: Accuracy category: Accurate (<=10%), Acceptable (10-25%), Poor (>25%), or Future.

                a=1, CALCULATE(Min(C_Dim_calendar[date_id]),FILTER(ALL(C_Dim_calendar),C_Dim_calendar[Year]=SELECTEDVALUE(C_Dim_calendar[Year]) && C_Dim_calendar[Month]=SELECTEDVALUE(C_Dim_calendar[Month]))),

measures.name: SLE_Waterfall_Capacity_Actual
measures.expression:
var mul = SELECTEDVALUE(SLE_Waterfall_Lineitem[Multiplier])
var lineitem= SELECTEDVALUE(SLE_Waterfall_Lineitem[Line_Item])
var std = CALCULATE([F_Start_date])
var wct =  {"PET", "APET", "RGB", "JUICE-BRICK", "CAN", "HDPE FG", "NRGB","CHIPS FG"}
var etd= CALCULATE([F_End_date])

var INS_CAP = CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[total_hours]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct))
var Paid_Time = CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[paid_hours]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct))


var res = SWITCH(lineitem,
"Installed Capacity",DIVIDE(INS_CAP,
INS_CAP)*mul,
"Non-scheduled Time",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[total_hours]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct))-CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[active_shift_hours]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct)),INS_CAP)*mul,
"Asset Utilization",[Asset_Capacity_Actual]*mul,
"Scheduled Maintenance",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[scheduled_maintenance]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct)),INS_CAP)*mul,
"Technical projects and validations",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[lineproduct_validation]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct)),INS_CAP)*mul,
"Lost Time",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[lost_time]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct)),INS_CAP)*mul,
"Output",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[theoretical_line_hours_rated_speed]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[bic_cwc_type] in wct)),INS_CAP)*mul,
"Paid Time",DIVIDE(Paid_Time,
INS_CAP) *mul,
"Maintenance and Set up",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[maintenance_and_setup]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"C/O and CIP",[CIP_Actual]*mul,
"EPL",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[epl]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"OPL",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[opl]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"Speed Losses",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[speed_losses]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"Minor Stoppages",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[minor_stoppages]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"System Line Efficiency",[Total_SLE_Actual]*mul,
BLANK())

return res

        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[opl]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"Speed Losses",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[speed_losses]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"Minor Stoppages",DIVIDE(CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe002,fact_pe002[minor_stoppages]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter]=1)),Paid_Time)*mul,
"System Line Efficiency",[Total_SLE_Actual]*mul,
BLANK())

return res

measures.expression:
VAR country = SELECTEDVALUE(C_Dim_Geography[Country])

VAR region = SELECTEDVALUE(Dim_Region[Region])

var std = CALCULATE([F_Start_date],FILTER(All(Selector_Time_Period),Selector_Time_Period[Time Period]="MTD"))
var etd= CALCULATE([F_End_date],FILTER(ALL(Selector_Time_Period),Selector_Time_Period[Time Period]="MTD"))
VAR Result = CALCULATE(
    SWITCH(
        TRUE(),
        NOT (ISBLANK(country)), CALCULATE(SUM(KESICSC33[act]), KESICSC33[Country] = country),
            switch(TRUE(),
                    NOT (ISBLANK(bu)), CALCULATE(SUM(KESICSC33[act]), KESICSC33[Business_Unit] = bu),
                        switch(TRUE(),
                                NOT (ISBLANK(region)), CALCULATE(SUM(KESICSC33[act]), KESICSC33[Region] = region),
        CALCULATE(SUM(KESICSC33[act]), KESICSC33[Month_Level] = "1")))),FILTER(ALL(C_Dim_calendar),C_Dim_calendar[date_id]<=etd && C_Dim_calendar[date_id]>=std))
    

RETURN if (ISBLANK(Result),0, DIVIDE(Result, 100, 0))

source.expression:
let
    Source = Table.FromRows(Json.Document(Binary.Decompress(Binary.FromText("i44FAA==", BinaryEncoding.Base64), Compression.Deflate)), let _t = ((type nullable text) meta [Serialized.Text = true]) in type table [Column1 = _t]),
    #"Changed Type" = Table.TransformColumnTypes(Source,{{"Column1", type text}})
in
    #"Changed Type"

let
    fact_actuals_View = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,
    "SELECT
    co012.process_run_id,
    co012.bic_chversion,
    co012.bic_chcurtype, 
    co012.fiscper,
    co012.comp_code,
    /*COALESCE(
           CASE WHEN 
    LEFT(bic_ccusthie4 ,5)='00000'
    THEN 
    RIGHT(bic_ccusthie4 ,5)
    ELSE 
    bic_ccusthie4  END
    , 'NA') AS bic_ccusthie4,*/

    sum(volume_in_transactions_ctr) as volume_in_transactions_ctr, 
    sum(volume_in_uc) as volume_in_uc, 
    sum(net_sales_revenue) as net_sales_revenue, 
    sum(gross_profit_other) as gross_profit_other, 
    sum(gross_profit_total) as gross_profit_total,
    sum(revenue_other) as revenue_other , -- Added revenue_other column
    sum(underlying_ebit) as ebit,
    sum(cogs_fees_and_taxes) as cogs_fees_and_taxes,
    sum(direct_cost_of_advertising_promotion) as dme,
    sum(production_overheads) as production_overheads,
    sum(haulage) as haulage,
    sum(warehouse_total) as warehouse_total,
    sum(distribution_and_delivery_total) as distribution_and_delivery_total,
    --avg(net_contribution) as avg_net_contribution,
    sum(net_contribution) as net_contribution,
    sum(total_input_cost) as total_input_cost
    --sum(total_depreciation) as total_depreciation,
   -- sum(total_taxesfees_in_nsr) as total_taxesfees_in_nsr

    FROM david_test_metrics.udm_datamart_fm.co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals as co012

    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material as mat
    ON co012.material = mat.material
    LEFT JOIN  david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer  as cust
    ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer  -- Join the customer table on the customer field
 
    WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype = '" & CurrencyFilter & "' 
AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
            co012.fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001') --co012.fiscper IN ('2025001')
        ELSE 
            co012.fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)
--AND  ( co012.comp_code IN('0563','0562') AND co012.fiscper  IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))

    GROUP BY
    co012.process_run_id,
    co012.bic_chversion,
    co012.bic_chcurtype,
    co012.fiscper,  
    co012.comp_code", 

    null, [EnableFolding=true]),
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(fact_actuals_View, RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else fact_actuals_View
in
    Result

let 
    Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh, "WITH plan_data AS (
    -- Fetch data from the co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_planning table
    SELECT 

        
        CASE 
          WHEN co012.bic_chversion IN ('R040', 'R070', 'R100') THEN 'RE'
          ELSE co012.bic_chversion 
        END AS bic_chversion,
        co012.bic_chcurtype,        
        co012.fiscper,    
        
        co012.comp_code,        
        SUM(volume_in_transactions_ctr) AS volume_in_transactions_ctr,  
        SUM(volume_in_uc) AS volume_in_uc,  
        SUM(net_sales_revenue) AS net_sales_revenue,  
        SUM(gross_profit_other) AS gross_profit_other,
        sum(co012.gross_profit_total),
        SUM(revenue_other) AS revenue_other  ,
    sum(underlying_ebit) as ebit,
    sum(direct_cost_of_advertising_promotion) as dme,
    sum(production_overheads) as production_overheads,
    sum(cogs_fees_and_taxes) as cogs_fees_and_taxes,
    sum(haulage) as haulage,
    sum(warehouse_total) as warehouse_total,
        sum(distribution_and_delivery_total) as distribution_and_delivery_total,
    avg(net_contribution) as avg_net_contribution,
    sum(net_contribution) as net_contribution,
    sum(total_input_cost) as total_input_cost,
    sum(total_depreciation) as total_depreciation,
    sum(total_taxesfees_in_nsr) as total_taxesfees_in_nsr
    
    FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_planning AS co012
    
    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material AS mat ON co012.material = mat.material
    
    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer  as cust
    ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer


    WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype = '30' 
--AND  ( co012.comp_code='0563' AND co012.fiscper IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))    
AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
            co012.fiscper IN ('2025001')
        ELSE 
            co012.fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)
      AND (
          co012.bic_chversion = 'B000'
          OR  (
              LEFT(co012.fiscper, 4) = CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING)  
              AND co012.bic_chversion = '"& RE_Version &"'
              )
          )
    
    GROUP BY 
        bic_chversion,
        co012.bic_chcurtype,        
        co012.fiscper,    
        
        co012.comp_code
), 

actual_data AS (
    -- Fetch data from the co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals table
    SELECT 
        
        'RE' AS bic_chversion,
        co012.bic_chcurtype,        
        co012.fiscper,    
        
        co012.comp_code,        
        SUM(volume_in_transactions_ctr) AS volume_in_transactions_ctr,  
        SUM(volume_in_uc) AS volume_in_uc,  
        SUM(net_sales_revenue) AS net_sales_revenue,  
        SUM(gross_profit_other) AS gross_profit_other,
        sum(co012.gross_profit_total),
        SUM(revenue_other) AS revenue_other,
        sum(underlying_ebit) as ebit,
    sum(direct_cost_of_advertising_promotion) as dme,
    sum(production_overheads) as production_overheads,
    sum(cogs_fees_and_taxes) as cogs_fees_and_taxes,
    sum(haulage) as haulage,
    sum(warehouse_total) as warehouse_total,
        sum(distribution_and_delivery_total) as distribution_and_delivery_total,
    avg(net_contribution) as avg_net_contribution,
    sum(net_contribution) as net_contribution,
    sum(total_input_cost) as total_input_cost,
    sum(total_depreciation) as total_depreciation,
    sum(total_taxesfees_in_nsr) as total_taxesfees_in_nsr
    
    FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals AS co012
    
    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material AS mat ON co012.material = mat.material

    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer  as cust
    ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer
    
    WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype =  '"& CurrencyFilter &"'
    --AND  ( co012.comp_code='0563' AND co012.fiscper IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))   
      AND (
        ('"& RE_Version &"' = 'R000' AND 1=0)  -- This will return no rows for R000
        OR ('"& RE_Version &"' = 'R040' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '003'))
        OR ('"& RE_Version &"' = 'R070' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '006'))
        OR ('"& RE_Version &"' = 'R100' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '009'))
      )
       GROUP BY 
        bic_chversion,
        co012.bic_chcurtype,        
        co012.fiscper,    
        co012.comp_code
)

SELECT * FROM plan_data
UNION ALL
SELECT * FROM actual_data", null, [EnableFolding=true]),

   #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(Source, RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else Source
in
    Result

 )
AND (

let
    fact_actuals_View = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,

" WITH summarized_data AS (
    SELECT
        CASE WHEN comp_code = '0452' THEN '0450' ELSE comp_code END AS comp_code,
        fiscper,
        (SUM(net_sales_revenue) + SUM(revenue_other)) AS nsr_10
    FROM
        david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals
    WHERE
        bic_chcurtype = 10 AND bic_chversion = '0000'
    GROUP BY
        CASE WHEN comp_code = '0452' THEN '0450' ELSE comp_code END, fiscper
),

fxn_rate_calculation AS (
    SELECT
        r1.comp_code,
        r1.fiscper,
        r1.nsr_10,
        COALESCE(r2.nsr_10, NULL) AS prev_year_nsr_10,
        COALESCE(r3.nsr_10, NULL) AS ppy_nsr_10,

        CASE
            WHEN r2.nsr_10 IS NOT NULL THEN r1.nsr_10 / r2.nsr_10
            ELSE NULL
        END AS fxn_rate,

        CASE
            WHEN r3.nsr_10 IS NOT NULL THEN r1.nsr_10 / r3.nsr_10
            ELSE NULL
        END AS fxn_rate_ppy

    FROM summarized_data r1
    LEFT JOIN summarized_data r2
        ON r1.comp_code = r2.comp_code
        AND SUBSTRING(r1.fiscper, 5, 3) = SUBSTRING(r2.fiscper, 5, 3)
        AND CAST(SUBSTRING(r1.fiscper, 1, 4) AS INT) = CAST(SUBSTRING(r2.fiscper, 1, 4) AS INT) + 1

    LEFT JOIN summarized_data r3
        ON r1.comp_code = r3.comp_code
        AND SUBSTRING(r1.fiscper, 5, 3) = SUBSTRING(r3.fiscper, 5, 3)
        AND CAST(SUBSTRING(r1.fiscper, 1, 4) AS INT) = CAST(SUBSTRING(r3.fiscper, 1, 4) AS INT) + 2
),

fxn_rates_table AS (
    SELECT
        comp_code,
        fiscper,
        nsr_10,
        prev_year_nsr_10,
        fxn_rate,
        ppy_nsr_10,
        fxn_rate_ppy
    FROM fxn_rate_calculation
    ORDER BY comp_code, fiscper
)

SELECT
    co012.bic_chversion,
    co012.bic_chcurtype,
    co012.fiscper,
    CASE WHEN co012.comp_code = '0452' THEN '0450' ELSE co012.comp_code END AS comp_code,
    sum(net_sales_revenue) as nsr_bev,
    sum(revenue_other) as nsr_other,
    sum(net_sales_revenue)/max(fxn_rate) as nsr_bev_fxn_py,
    sum(revenue_other)/max(fxn_rate) as nsr_other_fxn_py,
    sum(net_sales_revenue)/max(fxn_rate_ppy) as nsr_bev_fxn_ppy,
    sum(revenue_other)/max(fxn_rate_ppy) as nsr_other_fxn_ppy

FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals as co012

LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material as mat
    ON co012.material = mat.material

    LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer  as cust
    ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer

LEFT JOIN fxn_rates_table
    ON (
        CASE WHEN co012.comp_code = '0452' THEN '0450' ELSE co012.comp_code END = fxn_rates_table.comp_code
        AND co012.fiscper = fxn_rates_table.fiscper
    )

WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype = '"& CurrencyFilter &"'
--AND  ( co012.comp_code='0563' AND co012.fiscper IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))   
AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN
            co012.fiscper IN ('2024001')
        ELSE
            co012.fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)

GROUP BY
    co012.bic_chversion,
    co012.bic_chcurtype,
    co012.fiscper,
    CASE WHEN co012.comp_code = '0452' THEN '0450' ELSE co012.comp_code END
",
    null, [EnableFolding=true]),

    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(fact_actuals_View, RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else fact_actuals_View
in
    Result

let
    
    Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh, "
                WITH 
        cal_day_table AS (
            SELECT fiscper, fiscper_first_calday_ts
            FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals
            GROUP BY fiscper, fiscper_first_calday_ts
        ),
        
        plan_data AS (
            SELECT 
        co012.bic_chcurtype, 

        co012.fiscper, 

        co012.comp_code,

        CASE 
            WHEN co012.bic_chversion IN ('R040', 'R070', 'R100') THEN 'RE'
            ELSE co012.bic_chversion 
        END AS bic_chversion,
        SUM(net_sales_revenue) as net_sales_revenue,
        SUM(cogs_other) as cogs_other,
        sum(gross_profit_total) as gross_profit_total,
        SUM(gross_profit_other) as gross_profit_other

            FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_fxn_plan AS co012
            
            LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material AS mat 
                ON co012.material = mat.material
            
           LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer as cust
           ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer  

            LEFT JOIN cal_day_table 
                ON cal_day_table.fiscper = co012.fiscper


            WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype = '" & CurrencyFilter & "'
            --AND  ( co012.comp_code='0563' AND co012.fiscper IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))   
AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
            co012.fiscper IN ('2024001')
        ELSE 
            co012.fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)
            AND (
                co012.bic_chversion = 'B000'
                OR (
                    LEFT(co012.fiscper, 4) = CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING)
                    AND co012.bic_chversion = '" & RE_Version & "'
                )
            )
            GROUP BY 
        
        co012.bic_chcurtype, 
        co012.fiscper, 
        co012.comp_code,
        bic_chversion
        ),
        
        actual_data AS (
            SELECT 

        
        co012.bic_chcurtype, 

        co012.fiscper, 
        co012.comp_code,
        'RE' AS bic_chversion,
        SUM(net_sales_revenue) as net_sales_revenue,
        SUM(cogs_other) as cogs_other,
        sum(gross_profit_total) as gross_profit_total,
        SUM(gross_profit_other) as gross_profit_other

            
            FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_fm__co012_profitability_gross_profit_analysis_actuals AS co012
            
            LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_0_mat03_material AS mat 
                ON co012.material = mat.material
           
           LEFT JOIN david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_md__ca_0_cus03_customer as cust
           ON co012.customer_without_opex_allocation = cust.customer  

            LEFT JOIN cal_day_table 
                ON cal_day_table.fiscper = co012.fiscper
            
            WHERE co012.bic_chcurtype = '" & CurrencyFilter & "'
           -- AND  ( co012.comp_code='0563' AND co012.fiscper IN  ('2025008','2025001','2024008','2024001','2023008'))   
            AND (
                ('" & RE_Version & "' = 'R000' AND 1 = 0) 
                OR ('" & RE_Version & "' = 'R040' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '003'))
                OR ('" & RE_Version & "' = 'R070' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '006'))
                OR ('" & RE_Version & "' = 'R100' AND co012.fiscper BETWEEN CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '001') AND CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(CURRENT_DATE()) AS STRING), '009'))
            )
            GROUP BY 
        co012.bic_chcurtype, 
        co012.fiscper, 
        co012.comp_code,
        bic_chversion
        )

        SELECT * FROM actual_data
        UNION ALL
        SELECT * FROM plan_data
    ", null, [EnableFolding=true]),
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(Source, RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else Source
in
    Result

let
Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,"

let 

    Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,

    "
select distinct SNSH.country as country_sh,co_bu.country,BU_BU.business_unit,bic_ca_regn as Region,bic_ctframe,act,bp,py,fiscper,bic_ca_cntry

from (
select case when bu.txtlg='Ireland' then 'Island of Ireland' 
       when bu.txtlg='BU Ukr & Mol & Arm' then 'BU Ukr & Arm & Mol' else bu.txtlg end as BU,
       
       case when bic_ca_cntry='HR' then 'CR' 
       --when bu.txtlg='BU Ukr & Mol & Arm' then 'BU Ukr & Arm & Mol' 
       else bic_ca_cntry 
       end as bic_ca_cntry,
       SNP_Data.bic_cbunit,case when bic_ca_regn = '01' then 'REGION 1'
                                      when bic_ca_regn = '02' then 'REGION 2' 
                                      when bic_ca_regn = '03' then 'Italy'  else bic_ca_regn end as bic_ca_regn ,bic_ctframe,SNP_Data.country
,bic_kvalact as Act ,bic_kvalbptgt as BP,bic_kvalpy as PY,fiscper

from david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_cch_bw_mdm__ca_kbi050_ca_kbi_snapshots as SNP_Data left outer join 
      david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__bic_tcbunit_business_unit as BU on bu.bic_cbunit=SNP_Data.bic_cbunit 
where bic_csubkbi='KESICSC33'

) as SNSH left outer join 
      david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_dim_company as BU_BU on BU_BU.business_unit=SNSH.BU left outer join 
      david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_cchbc_md__ca_dim_company as CO_BU on CO_BU.country_abbreviation=SNSH.bic_ca_cntry
--select * from dc_datalake_prod_001.curated_cch_bw_mdm.ca_kbi050_ca_kbi_snapshots
    ", 


    null, 

    [EnableFolding=true]),
 
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(Source, RowLimit),

    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else Source,
    #"Added Custom" = Table.AddColumn(Result, "Month_Level", each if ([country_sh] = null) and ([country] = null) and ([business_unit] = null) and ([Region] = null) and ([bic_ca_cntry] = null) then 1 else 0),
    #"Filtered Rows" = Table.SelectRows(#"Added Custom", each ([country_sh] = null)),
    #"Removed Columns" = Table.RemoveColumns(#"Filtered Rows",{"country_sh"})
in
    #"Removed Columns"

Select comp_code ,fiscper,PE.material,
Sum(weight_g_sales_hidden) as weight_g_sales_hidden,
sum(sales_volume_in_bottles_number_in_millions) as sales_volume_in_bottles_number_in_millions,
sum(wa_pet_virgin_pb_plant_based_pct) as wa_pet_virgin_pb_plant_based_pct,

sum(pet_meltin_ob_oil_based_pct_hidden) as pet_meltin_ob_oil_based_pct_hidden,
sum(rpet_chip_pct_hidden) as rpet_chip_pct_hidden,
sum(rpet_flake_pct_hidden) as rpet_flake_pct_hidden

from david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_ftd__pe009_spr_recyclable_content PE

and comp_code not in ('0403','0550','0347','0307')
group by all

var NSR = [Total_NSR_Actuals]

return

measures.expression:
var x = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[KBI_Name])
var vol_sold = [Vol_UC_Actuals]
var NSR = [Total_NSR_Actuals]
var num_source = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[NUM_Source])
var den_source = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[DEN_Source])
var res = SWITCH(TRUE(),
            x="Vol Sold (UC)",vol_sold,
            x="Vol Produced (UC)",
            CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe004,fact_pe004[production_quantity_uc]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter] =1 )),
            x="NSR",NSR,
            x="NSR per UC",DIVIDE(NSR,vol_sold),
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee",[Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],vol_sold),
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],[Total_NSR_Actuals]),
            x="Haulage Cost",[Haulage Cost actuals],
            x="Haulage Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Haulage Cost actuals],vol_sold),
            x="Haulage Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Haulage Cost actuals],NSR),
            x="Distribution Cost",[Distribution Cost actuals],
            x="Distribution Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Distribution Cost actuals],vol_sold),
            x="Distribution Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Distribution Cost actuals],NSR),
            x="Warehouse Cost",[Warehouse Cost actuals],
            x="Warehouse Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Warehouse Cost actuals],vol_sold),
            x="Warehouse Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Warehouse Cost actuals],NSR),
            x= "Cost to Supply",[Cost to Supply Actuals],
            x="Cost to Supply per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Cost to Supply actuals],vol_sold),
            x="Cost to Supply as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Cost to Supply actuals],NSR),
            x= "Cost to Supply FXN",[Cost to Supply Actuals],
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes)",[Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes) per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],vol_sold),
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes) as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO Actuals BPC],NSR),
            x="Total SC Cost",[Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC],
            x="Total SC Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC],vol_sold),
            x="Total SC Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC],NSR),
            x= "Total SC Cost FXN",[Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC],

            BLANK())

return res

return res

measures.name: CL_VA_KBI_Wrapper_RE
measures.expression:
var x = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[KBI_Name])
var vol_sold = [Vol_UC_RE]
var NSR = [Total_NSR_RE]
var num_source = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[NUM_Source])
var den_source = SELECTEDVALUE(Mapping_KBI_Cost_Leadership[DEN_Source])
var res = SWITCH(TRUE(),
            x="Vol Sold (UC)",vol_sold,
            x="Vol Produced (UC)",
            CALCULATE(
        SUMX(fact_pe004,fact_pe004[planned_production_uc]),FILTER(Dim_wkctr,Dim_wkctr[CWC_Filter] =1 )),
            x="NSR",NSR,
            x="NSR per UC",DIVIDE(NSR,vol_sold),
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee",[Production Overhead PO RE BPC],
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO RE BPC],vol_sold),
            x="Production OH excl Env Fee as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO RE BPC],NSR),
            x="Haulage Cost",[Haulage Cost RE],
            x="Haulage Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Haulage Cost RE],vol_sold),
            x="Haulage Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Haulage Cost RE],NSR),
            x="Distribution Cost",[Distribution Cost RE],
            x="Distribution Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Distribution Cost RE],vol_sold),
            x="Distribution Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Distribution Cost RE],NSR),
            x="Warehouse Cost",[Warehouse Cost RE],
            x="Warehouse Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Warehouse Cost RE],vol_sold),
            x="Warehouse Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Warehouse Cost RE],NSR),
            x= "Cost to Supply",[Cost to Supply RE],
            x="Cost to Supply per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Cost to Supply RE],vol_sold),
            x="Cost to Supply as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Cost to Supply RE],NSR),
            x= "Cost to Supply FXN",[Cost to Supply RE],
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes)",[Production Overhead PO RE BPC],
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes) per UC Sold",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO RE BPC],vol_sold),
            x="Production Overhead (exc. fees and taxes) as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Production Overhead PO RE BPC],NSR),
            x="Total SC Cost",[Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC_RE],
            x="Total SC Cost per UC",DIVIDE([Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC_RE],vol_sold),
            x="Total SC Cost as % of NSR",DIVIDE([Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC_RE],NSR),
            x= "Total SC Cost FXN",[Supply Chain Cost Excl env_Fees_taxes_BPC_RE],

            BLANK())

return res

source.expression:
let
    Source = Table.FromRows(Json.Document(Binary.Decompress(Binary.FromText("i44FAA==", BinaryEncoding.Base64), Compression.Deflate)), let _t = ((type nullable text) meta [Serialized.Text = true]) in type table [Column1 = _t]),
    #"Changed Type" = Table.TransformColumnTypes(Source,{{"Column1", type text}})
in
    #"Changed Type"

let

Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,"select   bic_cconsunit, fiscper, bic_chcurtype, bic_chversion, bic_ccons_cat, bic_ccons_grp, fis_code, fis_code_parent, SUM(kbi_value) AS value 
from david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_bcs__bpc003_profit_and_loss_actuals
WHERE 

    bic_chcurtype = '30'

    AND bic_ccons_grp = 'G.LOCAL'

    AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
           fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')  --fiscper IN ('2024001', '2025001', '2023001')
        ELSE 
            fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)
   -- fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE())-2 AS STRING), '001')

    AND bic_chversion IN ('0000', 'B000', '" & RE_Version & "')
    

GROUP BY  bic_cconsunit, fiscper, bic_chcurtype, bic_chversion, bic_ccons_cat, bic_ccons_grp, fis_code, fis_code_parent", null, [EnableFolding=true]),
    #"Rename_RE_Version"= Table.TransformColumns(Source,{{"bic_chversion", each if Text.StartsWith(_,"R") then "RE" else _, type text}}),
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(#"Rename_RE_Version",RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else #"Rename_RE_Version"
in
    Result

let 

    Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,

    "WITH fact_bpc003_initial AS (
        SELECT 
            bic_cconsunit,fiscper,func_area,bic_chcurtype,bic_ccons_grp,bic_chversion,gl_account,kbi_value,
            hier_tab.hierarch_level,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                PARTITION BY bic_cconsunit, fiscper, func_area, bic_chcurtype, bic_ccons_grp, bic_chversion, gl_account
                ORDER BY hier_tab.hierarch_level DESC) AS row_num

        FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_bcs__bpc003_profit_and_loss_actuals AS bpc
             LEFT JOIN (SELECT bic_ccsfis, MAX(hierarch_level) AS hierarch_level
                        FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_md__bic_ccsitem_hierarchy
                         GROUP BY bic_ccsfis) AS hier_tab
              ON hier_tab.bic_ccsfis = bpc.fis_code
        WHERE 

            ((bic_chcurtype = 10 AND bic_ccons_grp = 'G.LOCAL')
            OR 
            (bic_chcurtype = 30 AND bic_ccons_grp = 'G.LOCAL'))
            and cast(left(fiscper,4 ) as INT) >= YEAR(CURRENT_DATE) - 3 and cast(left(fiscper,4 ) as INT) <= YEAR(CURRENT_DATE)

            AND bic_chversion in ('B000','0000')
            and bic_chcurtype ='30'
            and bic_ccons_grp='G.LOCAL'

),
ce_group_level as (

      SELECT distinct
            ce_group_level_1 ,ce_group_level_2,
            gl_account_range 
        FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_theedge_user_input_ca__opex_detailed_mapping
        WHERE 1=1
        and ce_group_level_1 <> ' '
        and ce_group_level_1= 'BUSINESS LOSSES'

)

    select cast(CONCAT(SUBSTRING(fiscper, 0, 4), '-', SUBSTRING(fiscper, 6, 2), '-01') as date) AS FormatedDate,bic_cconsunit as comp_code,cast(gl_account as int) as gl_account,
    fiscper,bic_chversion,func_area, bic_chcurtype,sum(kbi_value) as kbi_value
    FROM fact_bpc003_initial join ce_group_level on cast(gl_account as int) = gl_account_range

    WHERE row_num = 1
    AND (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
            fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001') 
        ELSE 
            fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001') 
    END
)
    group by all

    ", 

    null, 

    [EnableFolding=true]),
 
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(Source, RowLimit),

    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else Source,
 
    #"Filtered Rows" = Table.SelectRows(Result, each true),
    #"Replaced Value" = Table.ReplaceValue(#"Filtered Rows","C","",Replacer.ReplaceText,{"comp_code"}),
    #"Replaced Value1" = Table.ReplaceValue(#"Replaced Value",null,"F0",Replacer.ReplaceValue,{"func_area"})
in
    #"Replaced Value1"

let
    dim_calendar_view = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,

"SELECT *
        FROM david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__curated_theedge_user_input_ca__opex_detailed_mapping
        WHERE 1=1
        and ce_group_level_1 <> ' '
        and ce_group_level_1= 'BUSINESS LOSSES'
", 

null, [EnableFolding=true]),
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(dim_calendar_view,RowLimit),
    Result = if RowLimit > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else dim_calendar_view
in
    Result

let
Source = Value.NativeQuery(C_data_mesh,"
select * from david_test_metrics.test_schema.dc_datalake_prod_001__udm_datamart_rtr__hr_02m_manual_submission where kbi in (
     'khr004001_fte_total',
     'khr004014no_of_vacancies_gm',
     'khr004017_leased_fte',
     'khr004018total_absence_days_from_work',
     'khr004019standard_working_days',
     'khr004011_number_of_voluntary_permanent_leavers',
     'khr004012_number_of_involuntary_permanent_leavers', 
     'khr004013_actual_number_of_permanent_employees'
   ) and (
    CASE
        WHEN '"& FiscperFilter &"' = 'Sample' THEN 
            fiscper IN ('2025001')
        ELSE 
            fiscper >= CONCAT(CAST(YEAR(GETDATE()) - 2 AS STRING), '001')
    END
)

", null, [EnableFolding=true]),
    #"Kept First Rows" = Table.FirstN(Source ,#"RowLimit"),
    Result = if #"RowLimit" > 0 then #"Kept First Rows" else Source
